question: Return a table of delivered net revenue by gender and device_type, with columns gender, device_type, delivered_orders, and delivered_net_revenue.
sql_query: SELECT c.gender, o.device_type, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS delivered_orders, SUM(COALESCE(p.payment_sum, 0) - COALESCE(r.refund_sum, 0)) AS delivered_net_revenue FROM core.orders o JOIN core.customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id LEFT JOIN (SELECT order_id, SUM(payment_value) AS payment_sum FROM core.payments GROUP BY order_id) p ON o.order_id = p.order_id LEFT JOIN (SELECT oi.order_id, SUM(r.refund_amount) AS refund_sum FROM core.returns r JOIN core.order_items oi ON r.order_item_id = oi.order_item_id GROUP BY oi.order_id) r ON o.order_id = r.order_id WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered' GROUP BY c.gender, o.device_type ORDER BY c.gender, o.device_type
pandas_sql_query: SELECT c.gender, o.device_type, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) FILTER (WHERE o.order_status = 'delivered') AS delivered_orders, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN o.order_status = 'delivered' THEN oi.quantity * oi.unit_price - oi.discount_amount ELSE 0 END), 2) AS delivered_net_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o USING (customer_id) JOIN order_items oi USING (order_id) GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY gender, delivered_net_revenue DESC, device_type
diff_preview: [left]:  [female, female, female, male, male, male, non-binary, non-binary, non-binary]
[right]: [Female, Female, Female, Male, Male, Male, Non-binary, Non-binary, Non-binary]
